backlog.title: §7.1 Status-Line Hint Sync (copy-mode parity display fix)
backlog.description: Close the sole remaining gap from the copy-mode-parity delta: the TUI status line (renderStatus) still advertises the old conditional <S-sel> token. Update it to the always-shown static segment 'v=sel C-v=block o=swap' mandated by PRD §7.1, so the on-screen hints match the actual keybindings shipped in §7.4. This is a display-only change — it touches no tmux server, no shell-out, no IPC. All other delta items (§0.1 safety tooling, §7.4 selection logic, §17 decisions log) are already implemented and tested.

milestones.title: Sync the TUI status line to the copy-mode-parity keybindings
milestones.description: Update renderStatus() in src/tui/view.zig to emit the PRD §7.1 status-line format exactly: replace the conditional <S-sel> token (line 247) with a static 'v=sel C-v=block o=swap' segment. Update the four renderStatus unit tests, the stale comment in select.zig:45, and docs/CONFIGURATION.md to match. Verify the build and full test suite pass in ReleaseFast mode.

tasks.title: renderStatus emits 'v=sel C-v=block o=swap'; tests + CONFIGURATION.md updated
tasks.description: Modify the renderStatus() function in src/tui/view.zig to replace the conditional 'has_selection → <S-sel>' write (line 247) with an unconditional 'v=sel C-v=block o=swap' hint segment immediately before the existing 'Enter=render q=quit' static tail. Update the function doc comment (line 216), all four renderStatus unit tests (lines 713–800), the select.zig:45 comment that references the now-removed token, and docs/CONFIGURATION.md (Mode A doc-with-work) to reflect the new always-shown status-line shape. The has_selection field in the Status struct is RETAINED per delta §3 recommendation (minimal change; no region.zig churn).

subtasks.title: Replace <S-sel> conditional with static 'v=sel C-v=block o=swap' hint in renderStatus; update doc comment, 4 unit tests, select.zig comment, and CONFIGURATION.md
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_cd9cd575d6b1/architecture/codebase_findings.md for exact line numbers and before/after for every edit. The renderStatus() function is at src/tui/view.zig:226. The stale code is line 247: `if (status.has_selection) try w.writeAll("  <S-sel>");`. The existing static tail is line 249: `try w.writeAll("  Enter=render q=quit");`. The change is purely in these two lines.
2. INPUT: The Status struct (view.zig:84–91) already has all required fields. The has_selection field is RETAINED (set by region.zig:192 from ctx.sel.active()) but will no longer be READ by renderStatus after this change. No struct changes needed. The SelMode enum (.none/.line/.block) and the rest of the Status fields (cursor, pattern, matches) are unchanged.
3. LOGIC: Implement PRD §7.1 (selector h2.7/h3.8) status-line format. The function already builds the status line in a fixed buffer via std.Io.Writer.fixed and truncates to `cols`. The ONLY logic change is:
   (a) src/tui/view.zig line 247: REMOVE `if (status.has_selection) try w.writeAll("  <S-sel>");`
   (b) src/tui/view.zig line 249 (before the existing writeAll): ADD `try w.writeAll("  v=sel C-v=block o=swap");`
   The result: the always-shown tail reads `…  v=sel C-v=block o=swap  Enter=render q=quit`, matching §7.1 byte-for-byte.
   (c) src/tui/view.zig line 216 doc comment: replace `<S-sel>Enter=render q=quit` with `v=sel C-v=block o=swap  Enter=render q=quit`.
   (d) src/tui/select.zig line 45 comment: remove the stale `the status line's <S-sel> token +` phrase; update to `view.Status.has_selection (retained for future use)`.
   Mock nothing — this is a pure function test.
4. OUTPUT: renderStatus() output for [LINE], [BLOCK], and mode=.none all contain `v=sel C-v=block o=swap` and `Enter=render q=quit`, and contain NO `<S-sel>` token. The emitted status line matches PRD §7.1 exactly: `[LINE|BLOCK]  row:N col:M  /pattern  N match(es)  v=sel C-v=block o=swap  Enter=render q=quit`.
5. TESTS: Update the four renderStatus unit tests in src/tui/view.zig:
   - Test 'renderStatus: [LINE] full line' (line ~713): change line ~732 assertion from expecting `<S-sel>` present to expecting `v=sel C-v=block o=swap` present AND `<S-sel>` absent. The test sets has_selection=true; has_selection stays true in the Status struct — the hint is now always shown regardless.
   - Test 'renderStatus: [BLOCK] mode + field order' (line ~739): change line ~754 from asserting `<S-sel>` absent (because has_selection=false) to asserting `v=sel C-v=block o=swap` IS present (new hint is unconditional). Keep asserting `<S-sel>` absent.
   - Test 'renderStatus: mode=.none omits the bracket' (line ~758): ADD assertion that `v=sel C-v=block o=swap` is present (it is now always shown even in .none mode).
   - Test 'renderStatus: truncates the line to cols' (line ~779): No assertion change needed — it only checks line.len <= cols. But verify it still passes with the longer static tail (the truncation at cols=10 means 'row:1 col:1 ' is all that fits; the hint is truncated away, which is correct).
   Run `zig build test --release=fast` (MUST use --release=fast per PRD §15 — Debug mode hits a linker bug). All tests must pass. Also run `zig build --release=fast` to confirm clean build.
6. DOCS: [Mode A] Update docs/CONFIGURATION.md in THIS subtask (not a separate task):
   - Line ~114: Replace the format example `[LINE|BLOCK]  row:N col:M  /pattern  N match(es)  <S-sel>  Enter=render q=quit` with `[LINE|BLOCK]  row:N col:M  /pattern  N match(es)  v=sel C-v=block o=swap  Enter=render q=quit`.
   - Lines ~122–123: Replace the `<S-sel>` bullet (`<S-sel> — shown only when a selection is active.`) with a new bullet: `v=sel C-v=block o=swap — always shown; press v to start/re-anchor a linewise selection, Ctrl-v to toggle visual block mode, o to swap the cursor to the other end of the selection.`
   - Lines ~125–126: Update the 'nothing active' example from `row:1 col:1  Enter=render q=quit` to `row:1 col:1  v=sel C-v=block o=swap  Enter=render q=quit`.
   - The §7.4 keybinding table at docs/CONFIGURATION.md:~166 is ALREADY current — do NOT touch it.
   No external services to mock. No tmux integration required.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Sweep README.md and any other changeset-level overview docs to ensure they are consistent with the status-line hint change. The delta PRD §4 declares Mode B 'not applicable' because README.md:102 ('The in-app status line lists every key') is generic, but this task exists as the mandatory final safety net per the orchestrator's §5 rule to verify no overview doc is stale.

subtasks.title: Verify README.md and overview docs are consistent with the new status-line hint; update if stale
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: README.md:102 currently says 'The in-app status line lists every key' — this is generic and likely needs no change. However, README.md:100 says 'press v to toggle between linewise and block selection' which is slightly stale vs §7.4 (v begins/re-anchors linewise; Ctrl-v toggles block). Evaluate whether this one-liner needs a touch to avoid confusion. docs/CONFIGURATION.md was already updated in P1.M1.T1.S1 (Mode A); do NOT re-edit it here.
2. INPUT: The new status-line format from P1.M1.T1.S1 (renderStatus now emits `v=sel C-v=block o=swap Enter=render q=quit`). The §7.4 selection model: `v` begins/re-anchors linewise; `Ctrl-v` toggles block; `o` swaps ends.
3. LOGIC: Read README.md in full. If the region-overlay description (around line 98–103) accurately reflects the shipped §7.4 behavior and the new status line, make NO change and note 'README.md verified consistent — no update needed.' If any line is stale (e.g., 'v to toggle between linewise and block' misrepresents §7.4), update that line only to match the actual keybinding behavior (v=begin/re-anchor linewise, Ctrl-v=toggle block). Do NOT add new sections, feature lists, or capability blurbs — this is a one-line display fix.
4. OUTPUT: README.md is verified consistent with the shipped status-line format and §7.4 keybindings, or minimally updated if stale. Log the decision (changed / unchanged) in the task completion notes.
5. DOCS: This IS the documentation sync task — there is no further doc work after this.
